author: Dr. Priya Anand / Inpatient Pharmacy (PharmD Rachel Kim)
content:
MEDICATION RECONCILIATION — POD#4 (pre-discharge review)

HOME MEDICATIONS (confirmed with patient and daughter; patient keeps her own list):
- Lisinopril 20 mg PO daily (HTN) — held perioperatively; resume at discharge
- Amlodipine 10 mg PO daily (HTN) — continued
- Hydrochlorothiazide 25 mg PO daily (HTN) — held perioperatively; resume at discharge
- Metformin 1000 mg PO BID (T2DM) — held perioperatively; resume at discharge when eating reliably
- Atorvastatin 40 mg PO QHS (HLD) — continued
- Omeprazole 20 mg PO daily (GERD) — continued
- Calcium carbonate/vitamin D3 BID (osteopenia) — continued
- Fish oil 1000 mg daily (OTC) — patient preference

ANTITHROMBOTIC STATUS (explicitly verified):
- The patient is on NO therapeutic anticoagulant (no warfarin, no DOAC) and NO antiplatelet agent (no aspirin, no clopidogrel) at baseline or currently.
- The only parenteral agent during this admission is PROPHYLACTIC-dose enoxaparin 40 mg SubQ daily, started POD#1 for post-operative VTE prophylaxis. This is NOT stroke-prevention anticoagulation for atrial fibrillation.

ALLERGIES: Sulfa drugs (rash). No penicillin allergy.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Priya Anand, MD (PGY-2) and Rachel Kim, PharmD (Inpatient Pharmacy). 6/14/2027.
